Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> (Pron. "fuh REE.") Covers "local community reports, national [news] and state politics." Seeks more pitches on "overcoming adversity, why people do what they do, new ideas and personal stories of triumph." Is not interested in "stories that deal with the pessimistic view of the world " or "liberal vs. conservative discussions." Ferree's inside advice: "My advice to PR pros is to do what you are doing, making sure the info is correct and then continue to contact the person, never giving up until you have a definite 'no.'" Send him a press release, press kit, product sample or book. Prefers "email, fax, phone and mail," in that order. Calls are best Mon.-Fri., 9-10 a.m. CST.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> A public television and radio station, WFYI covers news happening out of Indiana. On the television side, the station produces several statewide public affairs programs about Indiana politics, business issues, cultural affairs and the local arts. On the radio side, relevant news stories in both Central Indiana and throughout the state receive coverage. "We also produce several radio series that deal with medicine, the Indiana legislature and the local arts community," Wright says. "Present us with timely and relevant news stories that would be of interest to central Indiana public radio listeners. Since WFYI Public Television does not have a news department, our television production unit is more interested in stories that can be told in long-form documentaries." Contact by email.
